options: He sweared that he had not stolen the bag.
He swore that he had not stolen the bag.
He was swearing that he had not stolen the bag.
He swears that he had not stolen the bag.
solution: 'Swore' is the correct Simple Past (irregular) form of 'swear'. 'Sweared' is not a valid word. The sentence narrates a past declaration, so past tense is required.
